title: membership — product analytics
description: Membership analytics feed churn + LTV + dunning-recovery dashboards. Financial-signal events (activated / renewed / lapsed / cancelled / refunded / charged-back) use consent tier 'essential' — the business needs these signals for revenue reporting regardless of user marketing consent. Behavioural events (trial_started / pass_consumed) use 'functional'. Marketing analytics (win-back conversion tracking) is deferred to growth::analytics.

events.name: Membership Created
events.description: Fires on MembershipCreated. Essential — revenue-signal for MRR forecasting.

events.name: Membership Lapsed
events.description: Terminal involuntary churn. Feeds win-back campaign eligibility (30-day trigger via growth::marketing).

events.name: Membership Charged Back
events.description: P1. Every chargeback tracked for compliance signal.

events.name: Trial Converted
events.description: High-value conversion event. Essential — funnel measurement.

notes: Analytics NEVER carry raw price_amount_cents, lifetime_value_cents, refund_amount — always bucketed (0, <50, <200, <1000, <5000, 5000+).
Dunning failure reasons categorised into ~10 buckets — never raw gateway messages (which may hint at customer PAN state via error text).
Cancellation reasons categorised into ~15 buckets — free-text cancellation_reason on the membership stays server-side; analytics gets the bucket only.
Marketing-consent-gated win-back campaigns live in growth::marketing — not tracked here. This module tracks revenue signals; growth::analytics tracks marketing outcomes.
